mission.title: alertmanager: Metric alertmanager_alerts reports "active" alerts when there are none
mission.description: Metric alertmanager_alerts reports "active" alerts when there are none. Requested by 15+ users.

steps.title: Check current alertmanager deployment
steps.description:
Verify your alertmanager version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
helm list -n alertmanager 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working alertmanager installation.

steps.title: Review alertmanager configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant alertmanager configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get configmap -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=alertmanager
```
**What did you do?**
Query multiple Prometheus instances for `alertmanager_alerts` to get the active alert count for each one.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Metric alertmanager_alerts reports "active" alerts when…
steps.description:
I've seen this discrepancy before too. IIRC the reason is that the metrics are computed based on the in-memory store of alerts which include the resolved ones. Those are deleted only every 30 minutes (by default). That being said, I agree that it would be nice if the numbers include only firing
```yaml
alertmanager_alerts{state="active"}
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n alertmanager -l app.kubernetes.io/name=alertmanager
kubectl get events -n alertmanager --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Metric alertmanager_alerts reports "active" alerts when…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I've seen this discrepancy before too. IIRC the reason is that the metrics are computed based on the in-memory store of alerts which include the resolved ones. Those are deleted only every 30 minutes (by default). That being said, I agree that it would be nice if the numbers include only firing alerts.

FWIW `suppressed` is the sum of inhibited and silenced alerts.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with alertmanager installed or the issue environment reproducible.
